__fixture__.name: read_only_local_source_badge_group
__fixture__.scenario: A local source file is the canonical object, but the current filesystem permission is read-only. The fixture keeps source-of-truth relation, write authority, dirty state, and freshness separate instead of treating every fact as a generic read-only label.

source_of_truth_relation.relation_label: The visible object is the canonical source file; only write permission is constrained.

write_authority.authority_summary: Aureline can inspect and compare this source file, but the filesystem permission blocks direct save.

dirty_state.dirty_summary: No unsaved changes are owned by this tab.

freshness.freshness_summary: The file identity and permission state are current for the local workspace.

reason_strip.cause_plain_language: This source file is opened from a read-only filesystem permission.
reason_strip.scope_plain_language: The restriction applies to this file in the current workspace checkout.
reason_strip.boundary_plain_language: The protected boundary is filesystem write authority, not source identity.
reason_strip.safe_next_step_plain_language: Duplicate the file to an editable copy or request write permission before saving.

canonical_source_relation_row.source_label: Current object is the canonical workspace source file.

next_safe_action.action_summary: Creates a new editable copy without mutating the read-only source file.

export_and_accessibility.accessible_name: Read-only canonical source file. The file is current, clean, and blocked only by filesystem write authority.
